summary: Weak static pattern match on auth guard; no traced exfiltration behavior
details: A a static rule matched on keyword co-occurrence (the tokens 'ping', 'GET', and 'id') within dist/guards/auth.guard.js of common-tg-service@1.3.233. These tokens are extremely common in NestJS-style auth guard code (HTTP verb strings, identifier fields, health-check endpoints) and their co-occurrence alone does not establish exfiltration. No hardcoded attacker C2, no environment scraping, no credential-path reads, no install-time lifecycle execution, and no traced code path binding the flagged tokens to a network exfiltration primitive were observed. Without corroborating semantic evidence tying a specific installer-side data source to a non-first-party destination, the pattern is consistent with ordinary framework code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
